# Trace to Reader

## Reader paragraph B-READER-001

Uses C1, C2, and K1. The paragraph frames the source as a recovery system for agent work rather than as a plan-template instruction.

## Reader paragraph B-READER-002

Uses C4 and K3. The paragraph compresses the operational workflow while preserving when each file is updated.

## Reader paragraph B-READER-003

Uses C5, C6, K3, and K4. The paragraph explains drift, lost findings, and repeated failure as the three design targets.

## Reader paragraph B-READER-004

Uses C2 and K1. The paragraph transfers the recovery principle to ReaderLab annotation and reading-package continuity.

## Reader paragraph B-READER-005

Uses C7, K5, and K7. The paragraph keeps host-specific shell and Claude plugin protocol out of ReaderLab architecture.

## Reader paragraph B-READER-006

Uses C3 and K6. The paragraph keeps the method bounded by task complexity and prevents workflow overhead on simple tasks.
